title: Relative Neighborhood
statement: Let $E \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and $a \in E$. A set $U \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ is a neighborhood of $a$ in $E$ if and only if there exists $\delta > 0$ such that $U = E \cap (a-\delta, a+\delta)$. Equivalently, every neighborhood of $a$ in $E$ is a set of the form $U_E(a) := E \cap (a-\delta, a+\delta)$ for some $\delta > 0$.
